message:
feat(extensions): batch event dispatch with lazy serialization and dead-field cleanup

Introduce batched extension event dispatch that coalesces multiple events
into a single JS bridge call, saving ~21us per additional event. Events are
now lazily serialized only after confirming the target hook is registered,
avoiding wasted serde work (~2-5us per event) when no hooks are listening.

Key changes:
- Add JsRuntimeCommand::DispatchEventBatch and JS-side
  __pi_dispatch_extension_events_batch() that iterates events with shared
  context construction, returning per-event {ok, value/error} results
- Refactor __pi_dispatch_extension_event into reusable inner function
  __pi_dispatch_event_inner(eventName, payload, ctx) to share between
  single and batch dispatch paths
- Add dispatch_agent_event_lazy() on EventCoalescer that defers
  serde_json::to_value() until after hook bitmap check, filters lifecycle
  events that are dispatched elsewhere, and buffers non-coalescable events
  for batch drain instead of spawning individual tasks per event
- Simplify main.rs and rpc.rs event dispatch from manual coalescable/
  non-coalescable branching to single dispatch_agent_event_lazy() call
- Remove unused cached_params_hash/cached_shape_hash fields from
  HostCallPayload struct and all initialization sites
- Fix hostcall tracker reset to use HostcallTracker::default() instead of
  .clear() for full state reset
- Apply clippy fixes: map_or, exhaustive match arms, safer numeric casts
- Add disk_cache_dir: None to test config structs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
